content.Text:
[Wed 2026-04-22 01:01 PDT] Create a new file `dashboard/canary.html` for AgentLens — an **Agent Canary Deployer** dashboard. This is a standalone HTML page (like the existing regression.html, compliance.html etc in the same directory).

## What it does
Interactive canary deployment simulator for AI agents. Users configure a canary rollout (traffic split, metrics thresholds) and the tool simulates real-time metric comparison between baseline and canary versions, then autonomously recommends promote/rollback.

## Technical requirements
- Single HTML file with `<link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">` for base theming
- All custom CSS in `<style>` block, all JS in `<script>` block  
- Uses CSS variables from styles.css: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--bg-secondary)`, `var(--bg-tertiary)`, `var(--text-primary)`, `var(--text-secondary)`, `var(--border)`, `var(--radius)`, `var(--radius-sm)`, `var(--accent)`, `var(--success)`, `var(--warning)`, `var(--danger)`
- Canvas-based charts (no external libraries)
- Back link to index.html in header

## Features

### 1. Canary Configuration Panel
- Agent name (text input)
- Baseline version / Canary version (text inputs, defaults v2.3.1 / v2.4.0)
- Traffic split slider (1%-50%, default 10%)
- Duration selector (5min, 15min, 30min, 1hr simulated)
- Metric thresholds: max error rate %, max latency increase %, min success rate %
- "Deploy Canary" button

### 2. Live Metrics Comparison (after deploy)
- Two-column layout: Baseline vs Canary
- Metrics tracked (simulated with realistic random data):
  - Success rate (%)
  - Error rate (%)
  - P50/P95/P99 latency (ms)
  - Token usage (avg per request)
  - Cost per request ($)
- Each metric shows current value + trend arrow (↑↓→)
- Color coding: green if canary is within thresholds, yellow if borderline (within 80-100% of threshold), red if exceeding

### 3. Real-time Canvas Chart
- Time series chart showing baseline vs canary error rate over time
- Two lines (blue=baseline, orange=canary) with filled area
- X-axis: time elapsed, Y-axis: error rate %
- Threshold line drawn as red dashed horizontal line
- Updates every 500ms during simulation

### 4. Autonomous Decision Engine
- Runs analysis after each data point
- Shows current recommendation: "🟢 PROMOTE — Canary outperforming baseline", "🟡 OBSERVING — Insufficient data", "🔴 ROLLBACK — Error rate exceeds threshold"
- Confidence percentage (increases over time as more data collected)
- Reasoning text explaining the decision factors
- Auto-decision: after enough data points (70%+ of duration), if confidence > 85%, show "Auto-action recommended" with countdown

### 5. Deployment History Table
- Shows previous canary deploys (simulated: 5 historical entries)
- Columns: Date, Agent, Versions, Duration, Traffic %, Outcome (Promoted/Rolled Back/Aborted), Key Finding
- Sortable by clicking column headers

### 6. Rollout Strategy Presets
- 3 preset buttons: Conservative (5% traffic, tight thresholds), Standard (10%), Aggressive (25%, loose thresholds)
- Clicking a preset fills in the config form

### 7. Export
- "Export Report" button generates JSON download of the canary analysis

## Layout
Follow the pattern of regression.html:
- `.canary-container` wrapper with padding/max-width
- Header with title "🐤 Agent Canary Deployer" and subtitle
- Stats grid at top (4 cards: Total Deploys, Promote Rate, Avg Canary Duration, Current Status)
- Config panel on left, live metrics on right (panel-row grid)
- Canvas chart below
- Decision engine panel below chart
- History table at bottom

## Simulation behavior
- When "Deploy Canary" is clicked, generate data points at intervals
- Baseline metrics: stable with small random noise
- Canary metrics: generally similar to baseline but with configurable drift
- 3 simulation scenarios (randomly chosen): canary-wins (canary slightly better), canary-neutral (similar), canary-fails (canary degrades over time)
- Simulation runs for the configured duration (compressed: 1hr = ~30 seconds real time)
- "Stop & Rollback" button available during simulation

Make it polished and professional. ~500-800 lines of HTML/CSS/JS.
I’m locating the existing dashboard pages and reading the patterns from `regression.html` / `compliance.html` first so the new page matches the structure and theme.
